turns.content: What brings you in today?

Well, doctor, I've been having this dizziness for about two or three weeks now. It happens when I stand up, and yesterday I almost fell. My wife also told me that sometimes my hand shakes. I'm a bit worried, and I also get headaches and feel tired a lot.
When did this start?
I'd say about three weeks ago. I remember it started after my grandson's birthday party. At first, I thought maybe I just stood up too fast, but it keeps happening.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

Well, it's not really a sharp pain. The dizziness feels like a spinning or like I'm about to faint. I also feel a numbness in my feet sometimes, and the headache is a kind of pressure at the back of my head. The shaking is just in my right hand, especially when I'm tired.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, no fever or chills. Just the dizziness and the other things I told you. I haven't felt hot or cold like that.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take metoprolol every morning for my blood pressure, and atorvastatin for my cholesterol. I think the metoprolol is 50 milligrams, and the atorvastatin is 20. I've been on them for a few years now.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

Yes, I have coronary artery disease and high blood pressure. That's why I take those medications. I also had a stent put in about a year ago after a small heart scare, but everything has been fine since then, until now.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Not that I know of. I've never had a bad reaction to any medicine. The only things I take are the metoprolol and the atorvastatin, and they've been fine.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I've been trying to sit down when I feel dizzy, and I drink more water because my wife thought maybe I was dehydrated. Sometimes I also rest more, but it doesn't really fix it. The dizziness still comes when I stand up.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Sitting or lying down helps a bit, but standing up always makes it worse. The shaking in my hand seems worse when I'm tired or after I've been busy. Walking for a while also makes the fatigue and dizziness come back.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's made me scared to do things on my own. I used to walk to the store every day, but now I don't trust myself. My wife gets worried if I get up too fast. I feel tired and frustrated because I can't do my gardening like before.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I quit smoking about ten years ago, after my heart trouble. I do have a glass of wine with dinner sometimes, maybe two or three times a week. Nothing more than that.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

Not that I know of. My father had heart problems too, but not this kind of dizziness or shaking. My mother had high blood pressure, but she never mentioned these symptoms. So I don't think it runs in the family.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, never. This is all new to me. After my heart procedure last year, I felt fine. But this dizziness and shaking is different. That's why I'm worried now—it doesn't feel like my usual heart problem.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, I think so. You're saying the dizziness when I stand up might be linked to my blood pressure medicine. But I'm still not sure about the shaking in my hand or the numbness in my feet. Is that all connected? I'm a bit confused.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically, I'd say maybe a 4 out of 10. The dizziness isn't constant, but when it hits, it's strong. Emotionally, it's more like a 7 or
Do you have any questions for me?
Yes, I do. Is there something wrong with my nerves or my brain? And what should I do if I feel dizzy again? I'm worried about falling and hurting myself.
What do you think might be going on?

Honestly, I'm not sure. I thought maybe it was my blood pressure acting up, or maybe something with my nerves. But I'm scared it could be something like a stroke, because of the shaking and numbness. I just hope it's not too serious.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Oh, one more thing. I've been having trouble sleeping lately. I wake up a lot at night, and I feel restless. Maybe that's part of why I'm so tired all day. And the headache is worse in the morning, when I first wake up.
